title: heterotheca sessiliflora var. fastigiata
intro: Heterotheca sessiliflora var. fastigiata, also known as the tall goldenweed, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family, native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its chemical composition of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Heterotheca sessiliflora var. fastigiata is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
